---
title: "SEC News Digest — Enforcement Actions"
subtitle: "Historical enforcement data extracted from SEC News Digest archives (1956–2014)"
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: github
        href: https://github.com/CasparDP
    scrolling: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---


```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(duckdb)
library(plotly)
library(DT)
library(scales)
library(lubridate)

# Connect to DuckDB (read-only)
db_path <- here::here("data/processed/sec_digest.duckdb")
con <- dbConnect(duckdb(), dbdir = db_path, read_only = TRUE)

# Helper: check if table exists
table_exists <- function(con, name) {
  name %in% dbListTables(con)
}

has_enforcement_data <- all(
  c("enforcement_actions", "respondents", "sanctions", "violations") %in%
    dbListTables(con)
)
```

# Overview

## Row {height=20%}

```{r value-boxes}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Actions"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  n_actions <- tbl(con, "enforcement_actions") |> count() |> pull(n)
} else {
  n_actions <- "—"
}
list(icon = "shield-exclamation", color = "primary", value = n_actions)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Digests Parsed"
n_parsed <- tbl(con, "parsing_results") |>
  filter(parsing_status %in% c("completed", "skipped")) |>
  count() |> pull(n)
list(icon = "file-earmark-text", color = "success", value = n_parsed)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Unique Respondents"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  n_resp <- tbl(con, "respondents") |>
    summarise(n = n_distinct(name)) |> pull(n)
} else {
  n_resp <- "—"
}
list(icon = "people", color = "warning", value = n_resp)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Settlement Rate"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  rate <- tbl(con, "enforcement_actions") |>
    summarise(
      settled   = sum(as.integer(settlement == TRUE), na.rm = TRUE),
      total     = sum(as.integer(!is.na(settlement)))
    ) |>
    collect() |>
    mutate(rate = if_else(total > 0, percent(settled / total, accuracy = 1), "—")) |>
    pull(rate)
} else {
  rate <- "—"
}
list(icon = "handshake", color = "info", value = rate)
```

## Row {height=80%}

### Col {width=60%}

```{r actions-over-time}
#| title: "Enforcement Actions Over Time"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  actions_by_month <- tbl(con, "enforcement_actions") |>
    mutate(month = floor_date(digest_date, "month")) |>
    count(month) |>
    collect() |>
    mutate(month = as.Date(month))

  p <- ggplot(actions_by_month, aes(x = month, y = n)) +
    geom_line(color = "#0d6efd", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "#dc3545",
                linewidth = 0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Actions per month") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("x", "y")) |>
    layout(hovermode = "x unified")
} else {
  plotly_empty() |>
    layout(
      title = list(text = "No enforcement data loaded yet", x = 0.5),
      annotations = list(
        text = "Run scripts 04 and 05 to extract and load enforcement actions.",
        xref = "paper", yref = "paper", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
        showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 14)
      )
    )
}
```

### Col {width=40%}

```{r action-type-donut}
#| title: "Action Types"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  action_types <- tbl(con, "enforcement_actions") |>
    count(action_type) |>
    collect()

  plot_ly(
    action_types,
    labels = ~str_to_title(action_type),
    values = ~n,
    type   = "pie",
    hole   = 0.45,
    marker = list(colors = c("#0d6efd", "#198754", "#dc3545")),
    textinfo = "label+percent",
    hovertemplate = "%{label}: %{value} actions<extra></extra>"
  ) |>
    layout(showlegend = TRUE,
           legend = list(orientation = "h", y = -0.1))
} else {
  plotly_empty() |> layout(title = list(text = "No data yet"))
}
```

# Respondents & Sanctions

## Row

### Col {width=50%}

```{r sanction-types}
#| title: "Sanction Types"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  sanctions_summary <- tbl(con, "sanctions") |>
    filter(!is.na(sanction_type)) |>
    count(sanction_type, sort = TRUE) |>
    collect() |>
    mutate(
      sanction_type = str_replace_all(sanction_type, "_", " ") |> str_to_title(),
      sanction_type = fct_reorder(sanction_type, n)
    )

  p <- ggplot(sanctions_summary, aes(x = n, y = sanction_type,
                                      text = paste0(sanction_type, ": ", n))) +
    geom_col(fill = "#0d6efd", width = 0.7) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
    labs(x = "Count", y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
} else {
  plotly_empty() |> layout(title = list(text = "No data yet"))
}
```

### Col {width=50%}

```{r entity-types}
#| title: "Respondent Types"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  entity_summary <- tbl(con, "respondents") |>
    mutate(entity_type = coalesce(entity_type, "unknown")) |>
    count(entity_type, sort = TRUE) |>
    collect() |>
    mutate(entity_type = str_to_title(entity_type))

  plot_ly(
    entity_summary,
    labels = ~entity_type,
    values = ~n,
    type   = "pie",
    hole   = 0.4,
    marker = list(colors = c("#0d6efd", "#ffc107", "#6c757d")),
    textinfo = "label+percent",
    hovertemplate = "%{label}: %{value}<extra></extra>"
  ) |>
    layout(showlegend = TRUE,
           legend = list(orientation = "h", y = -0.1))
} else {
  plotly_empty() |> layout(title = list(text = "No data yet"))
}
```

## Row

```{r top-respondents-table}
#| title: "Top Respondents (by action count)"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  top_resp <- tbl(con, "respondents") |>
    count(name, entity_type, sort = TRUE) |>
    collect() |>
    rename(
      Respondent   = name,
      Type         = entity_type,
      Actions      = n
    ) |>
    mutate(Type = coalesce(Type, "unknown") |> str_to_title()) |>
    head(100)

  datatable(
    top_resp,
    rownames    = FALSE,
    filter      = "top",
    extensions  = "Buttons",
    options     = list(
      pageLength = 15,
      dom        = "Bfrtip",
      buttons    = c("csv", "excel"),
      scrollX    = TRUE
    )
  )
} else {
  cat("No enforcement data loaded yet. Run scripts 04 and 05 first.")
}
```

# Violations

## Row

### Col {width=60%}

```{r top-statutes}
#| title: "Most-Cited Statutes"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  statutes <- tbl(con, "violations") |>
    filter(!is.na(statute)) |>
    count(statute, sort = TRUE) |>
    collect() |>
    head(20) |>
    mutate(statute = fct_reorder(statute, n))

  p <- ggplot(statutes, aes(x = n, y = statute,
                             text = paste0(statute, ": ", n, " citations"))) +
    geom_col(fill = "#198754", width = 0.7) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
    labs(x = "Citations", y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
} else {
  plotly_empty() |> layout(title = list(text = "No data yet"))
}
```

### Col {width=40%}

```{r settlements-by-type}
#| title: "Settlement Rate by Action Type"
if (has_enforcement_data) {
  settle_by_type <- tbl(con, "enforcement_actions") |>
    filter(!is.na(settlement)) |>
    count(action_type, settlement) |>
    collect() |>
    group_by(action_type) |>
    mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) |>
    filter(settlement == TRUE) |>
    mutate(action_type = str_to_title(action_type))

  p <- ggplot(settle_by_type,
              aes(x = action_type, y = pct,
                  text = paste0(action_type, ": ", percent(pct, accuracy = 1)))) +
    geom_col(fill = "#0d6efd", width = 0.5) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Settlement rate") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
} else {
  plotly_empty() |> layout(title = list(text = "No data yet"))
}
```

# Pipeline Coverage

## Row {height=25%}

```{r coverage-boxes}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Files on Disk"
# "completed" = downloaded this run, "skipped" = already existed on disk.
# Both mean the file is available. "failed" = 404 (weekend/holiday, no digest published).
n_downloaded <- tbl(con, "download_manifest") |>
  filter(download_status %in% c("completed", "skipped")) |>
  count() |> pull(n)
list(icon = "download", color = "success", value = scales::comma(n_downloaded))
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Years in Manifest"
n_years <- tbl(con, "download_manifest") |>
  summarise(n = n_distinct(year)) |> pull(n)
list(icon = "calendar-range", color = "primary", value = n_years)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Parse Failures"
n_failed <- tbl(con, "parsing_results") |>
  filter(parsing_status == "failed") |>
  count() |> pull(n)
list(icon = "exclamation-triangle", color = if (n_failed > 0) "danger" else "success",
     value = n_failed)
```

## Row {height=75%}

### Col

```{r coverage-heatmap}
#| title: "Files on Disk by Year"
# on_disk   = completed + skipped (file present locally)
# attempted = on_disk + failed (dates the scraper actually tried; excludes pending)
# coverage  = on_disk / attempted  (~70% is the realistic ceiling: ~30% of dates are
#             weekends/holidays where SEC never published, so those will always 404)
coverage <- tbl(con, "download_manifest") |>
  count(year, download_status) |>
  collect() |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(
    on_disk   = sum(n[download_status %in% c("completed", "skipped")]),
    failed    = sum(n[download_status == "failed"]),
    pending   = sum(n[download_status == "pending"]),
    attempted = on_disk + failed,
    coverage_pct = if_else(attempted > 0, on_disk / attempted, NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p <- ggplot(coverage, aes(
    x    = year,
    y    = coalesce(coverage_pct, 0),
    text = paste0(
      year, ": ",
      if_else(!is.na(coverage_pct), percent(coverage_pct, accuracy = 1), "not downloaded"),
      "\n", on_disk, " files on disk / ", attempted, " dates tried",
      if_else(pending > 0, paste0("\n(", pending, " pending)"), "")
    )
  )) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = coverage_pct), width = 0.8, na.rm = FALSE) +
  # grey out years not yet downloaded
  geom_col(data = filter(coverage, is.na(coverage_pct)),
           aes(x = year, y = 0.05), fill = "#dee2e6", width = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#ffc107", high = "#198754", na.value = "#dee2e6",
                      labels = percent_format(), name = "On disk\n(of tried)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1956, 2014, 5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Files on disk / dates tried",
       caption = "Denominator = dates actually attempted (excludes pending). ~30% of dates are weekends/holidays (expected 404s), so ~70% is the realistic ceiling.") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "right")

ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") |>
  layout(hovermode = "closest")
```

### Col

```{r parse-quality}
#| title: "Parsing Quality by Year"
parse_by_year <- tbl(con, "parsing_results") |>
  left_join(tbl(con, "download_manifest") |> select(local_path, year),
            by = c("pdf_path" = "local_path")) |>
  filter(!is.na(year)) |>
  count(year, parsing_status) |>
  collect() |>
  mutate(year = as.integer(year)) |>
  group_by(year) |>
  mutate(total = sum(n), pct = n / total) |>
  filter(parsing_status %in% c("completed", "skipped")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  arrange(year)

if (nrow(parse_by_year) > 0) {
  p <- ggplot(parse_by_year, aes(x = year, y = pct,
                                  text = paste0(year, ": ", percent(pct, accuracy = 1)))) +
    geom_line(color = "#0d6efd", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_point(color = "#0d6efd", size = 2) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1956, 2014, 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(x = "Year", y = "Parse success rate") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
} else {
  plotly_empty() |>
    layout(
      title = list(text = "No parsing data yet", x = 0.5),
      annotations = list(
        text = "Run script 02 to parse downloaded digest files.",
        xref = "paper", yref = "paper", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
        showarrow = FALSE, font = list(size = 14)
      )
    )
}
```

```{r cleanup}
dbDisconnect(con)
```
